title: Van Ness Avenue Complete Streets — Phase 1
description: Redesign of Van Ness Avenue from Market Street to Jackson Street (2.3km) from a 4-lane arterial to a Complete Streets corridor with dedicated Bus Rapid Transit lanes, protected cycle tracks, enhanced pedestrian crossings, and street trees.
rawText:
PROPOSAL: Van Ness Avenue Complete Streets Project — Phase 1

BUDGET: $45,000,000 (SF Capital Budget FY2025)

DURATION: 18 months (commencement January 2025, completion June 2026)

JUSTIFICATION: Van Ness Avenue carries 45,000 vehicles per day and is a primary SFMTA Muni corridor. Current configuration causes peak-hour delays averaging 28 minutes for bus riders. Complete Streets redesign will add dedicated BRT lanes reducing transit times by 40%, protected cycle tracks, widened sidewalks, and 6 upgraded bus shelters.

CONTRACTOR: To be selected via SFPUC competitive bid process. Pre-qualified contractors include Bay Area Infrastructure Inc., DPW General Contractors LLC, and Golden Gate Civil Works.

IMPACT ASSESSMENT: Full CEQA review completed December 2024. Community outreach conducted across 14 neighborhood associations. Environmental impact: removal of 12 mature street trees (all to be replaced 3:1).

SPECIAL NOTES: Protected cycling infrastructure to be installed for first time on Van Ness. Utility undergrounding for 2.3km of overhead lines included in scope. Business disruption fund of $2.5M established for affected Van Ness corridor businesses.
targetArea: Van Ness Avenue, San Francisco, CA
extractedArea: Van Ness Avenue, San Francisco — 2.3km Market St to Jackson St
